content.problemStatement:
There are KKK magical pens (numbered 111 through KKK). You are given strings P1,P2,…,PKP_1, P_2, \ldots,  P_KP1​,P2​,…,PK​  (each of which consists of characters from **'a', 'b', ..., 't'**) ; for each valid iii, the iii-th pen can only write letters from the string PiP_iPi​.

You want to write a word SSS of length NNN. All the characters of SSS are  between **'a'** and **'t'** inclusive. This string must be written from left to right. To write it, you pick up some pen and start writing; after you've written some prefix of SSS, you can put down that pen, pick up another pen, continue writing SSS from the point where you put down the previous pen, later pick up another pen (any pen) and continue writing SSS with that pen, and so on until you write the whole string SSS. You may pick up each pen any number of times, including zero.

You have to find a way of writing the word SSS such that the number of times you change the pen (put down the pen you're currently writing with and pick up another) is the smallest possible. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one. It is guaranteed that it is possible to write SSS with the given pens.
constraints: S,P1,P2,…,PKS, P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_KS,P1​,P2​,…,PK​ contain only characters **'a', 'b', ..., 't'**
For each valid iii, all characters of PiP_iPi​ are pairwise distinct
The sum of lengths of all the strings on the input does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains a single string SSS.
For each valid iii, the iii-th of the next KKK lines contains a single string PiP_iPi​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the number of the pen with which you want to write the iii-th character of SSS.

Your output will be considered correct if each character can be written with the pen you want to write it with and the number of times you have to change the pen you are writing with is minimum possible.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
4 2
abcd
ab
cd
4 2
baab
ab
ca
4 2
acaa
ab
cd
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: You can write the first two characters with the first pen and the next two characters with the second pen. This means you have to change the pen once (from pen 111 to pen 222).
Example case 2: You can write the whole string SSS with the first pen, so you never have to change the pen you are writing with.
Example case 3: You can write the first character with the first pen, the second character with the second pen and then the last two characters again with the first pen. Thus, you have to change the pen twice (from pen 111 to pen 222 and then from pen 222 to pen 111).

hidden.input:
3
4 2
abcd
ab
cd
4 2
baab
ab
ca
4 2
acaa
ab
cd
